copy.title: Astros demolish Oakland. Leclerc wins Monaco. Spurs-Knicks Game 3 tomorrow.
copy.keyTakeaway: Knicks are one win from the title after taking Game 2 at home. Tech stocks got crushed on a down market day. F1 heads to Canada after Leclerc's Monaco win.

todaysHits.sports: Knicks survive Game 2, lead Spurs 2-0 in Finals.
todaysHits.golf: Gerard leads Memorial at 9-under, still playing.
todaysHits.f1: Leclerc wins Monaco; Verstappen third, Hamilton second.
todaysHits.worldcup: World Cup 2026 kicks off June 11 in North America.
todaysHits.culture: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce planning July wedding at MSG.

lead.headline: Houston dismantled Oakland 13-2. Not even close.
lead.whatHappened: The Astros offense was in a different class Sunday, putting up double digits while the Athletics' bats stayed completely silent. This was a blowout that looked lopsided from the first inning.
lead.whyBullet1: Houston's dominance at home sets a dangerous tone heading into the week—they're the team to beat in their division right now.
lead.whyBullet2: Oakland's offense has real problems, and a loss this bad suggests deeper issues than one bad game.
lead.whatToSay: If you're watching Houston down the stretch, they just showed you why people think they're dangerous.

sportsOther: Astros blew out Oakland by 11 — that's a statement win in June.
Cardinals edged Cincinnati in a tight one, nothing decided early.
Dodgers dominated the Angels in the crosstown matchup, no real contest.

markets.mood: Tech stocks got hammered on a broad selloff, with the Nasdaq down 4.8% as the 10-year Treasury yield jumped 1.9% for the week, making bonds actually competitive again.
markets.whyBullet1: When Treasury yields spike that fast, growth stocks suffer because their future earnings are worth less in today's dollars. Nvidia, Tesla, and Dell all dropped 6%+ because they're priced on the assumption of cheap money that just got more expensive.
markets.whyBullet2: The inflation print on Thursday will tell you whether this selloff was justified or overdone. If CPI stays hot, the Fed stays stubborn, and yields keep climbing. If it cools, tech gets a chance to stabilize.
markets.bringUp: Bitcoin's up 3% while stocks tank, which is the first time in months it's decoupled from the Nasdaq in a down market. That's traders hedging against continued volatility or rotating to non-correlated assets.

golf.headline: Gerard and Poston tied at Muirfield Village heading into final round
golf.whyCare1: Memorial is one of golf's most prestigious events and a crucial FedEx Cup checkpoint before the playoffs.
golf.whyCare2: Muirfield Village is Jack Nicklaus's course and plays brutally tough — scoring at nine under is legitimately impressive.
golf.watchFor: Whether Ryan Gerard can hold the lead Sunday or if J.T. Poston makes a move on the back nine.
golf.whatToSay: Gerard's flying under the radar but he's right there with Poston — could be a serious final round.

f1.headline: Leclerc wins Monaco. Hamilton second. Verstappen third.
f1.whyCare1: Leclerc's home circuit victory keeps Ferrari competitive in the championship fight.
f1.whyCare2: Hamilton's podium finish shows Mercedes can challenge at Monaco despite setup constraints on tight street circuits.
f1.watchFor: Whether Verstappen's third-place result signals a temporary dip or a broader pattern as the season progresses.
f1.whatToSay: Leclerc finally delivered at home. Monaco always rewards the driver who nails the one qualifying lap that matters.

culture.topic: Knicks take 2-0 series lead over Spurs
culture.whatHappened: New York won Games 1 and 2 of the NBA Finals against San Antonio, with the Knicks getting some favorable late-game calls and executing in crucial moments.
culture.whyItMatters: The Knicks are one win away from their first championship since 1970, and MSG ticket prices for Games 3 and 4 just jumped hard — meaning the city actually believes this is happening.
culture.whatToSay: If the Knicks pull this off, New York loses its mind for a week straight.

culture.topic: Anthony Head, Buffy's Giles, dead at 72
culture.whatHappened: The British actor who played the headmaster and father figure on Buffy the Vampire Slayer passed away, and the cast released a joint tribute.
culture.whyItMatters: Giles was the moral center of that show — the guy who actually gave a damn — and losing him hits different if you grew up with that series.
culture.whatToSay: Giles made you want to read more and take yourself less seriously at the same time.

culture.whatHappened: The show about surgical work-life separation — employees literally split their memories at the office door — came back and picked up exactly where it left off.

copy.finalSharpTake: Tech got crushed today—QQQ down 4.8%, NVDA and TSLA both off 6.6%—but the real story is the 10-year Treasury climbing 1.9% on the week while stocks barely moved. Bond traders and equity traders are no longer on the same page about where rates go from here. Baseball was a bloodbath too, Houston demolished Oakland 13–2. Tomorrow the Spurs and Knicks play Game 3 of the Finals.
